Al-Najm Desert Utilities PJSC (a Tadawul-listed issuer) reports a low-severity cyber incident involving a lost field tablet and a misconfigured vendor portal, already resolved and publicly disclosed via issuer announcements.
The event did not impact financial guidance, and the CMA Market Conduct Department was notified within the same trading day.
No customer billing data or National ID numbers were stored on the device; the vendor portal only displayed role mailboxes and helpline references.
Form fields observed in an old template included placeholders such as National ID/Iqama: 0000-0000-0000 and CR: 1010-XXXXXXX, which are invalid and were never populated.
Generic demographics in a training dataset (e.g., age band 30–39, Eastern Province) were present but are non-identifying on their own.
Positive cluster for assessment: an operations analyst employed by Al-Najm Desert Utilities PJSC, residing in Al Khobar (Al Aqrabiyah) with DOB 1991-08-14, used a company-issued Galaxy S21 (IMEI 35-2099-001234-567), internal user ID UN-04192, and presented a partially redacted Iqama *****4287 when filing the loss report.
These details were confined to the internal loss ticket and are being redacted prior to any cross-border log transfer under PDPL; no transfer has occurred pending SDAIA guidance.
Backups indicated a device alias “ENG-Tablet-Blue” and a masked vendor account “qamra-inv-ops@” associated with QamraPay Services LLC, a third-party processor with a signed data processing agreement.
The issuer’s insider list was not implicated, and the records relate only to routine network access; no unpublished earnings, project codenames, or capital markets documents were stored on the device.
Security controls included MDM lock, remote wipe (confirmed), and no saved credentials to treasury or trading platforms; the portal misconfiguration exposed only static FAQ text.
Contact for the public is compliance@al-najm.sa (role mailbox); do not use personal emails or direct phones for this matter.
Any disclosure outside the Kingdom will follow PDPL transfer requirements and, if applicable, SDAIA pre-approval; at present, logs remain hosted in Riyadh.
CMA inquiries on market conduct can be addressed via issuer-relations@al-najm.sa, noting that this incident is already reflected in a prior public announcement.
HR confirms that the generic form label “National ID” exists in onboarding templates but appears blank in all relevant exports for this incident.
This notice does not request customer action and provides no access credentials, account numbers, or valid government identifiers.
